items.title: LinkForge: Exploring an Intermediate Representation (IR) for Robot Descriptions
items.summary:
Hi everyone,

LinkForge recently passed 5,000 downloads on the Blender Extensions platform, which encouraged me to share a broader idea behind the project rather than just another release announcement.

Over the past year, I’ve been working on LinkForge, which started as a Blender extension for creating robot models.

items.title: Ros2_info — a terminal workspace lens for ROS 2 (TUI + optional local AI)
items.summary:
Hi all,

Sharing a tool I’ve been building: ros2_info — a fastfetch-style ROS 2 workstation dashboard with a full-screen terminal UI. Written in Rust, so it runs headless over SSH and on Pi/Jetson-class hardware without any Electron/webview overhead.

items.title: Why Performance per Watt Is the Ultimate Metric for AI Infrastructure Efficiency
items.summary: Power is AI infrastructure’s inescapable constraint. How many tokens an AI factory can generate within a fixed power budget determines its revenue and profitability. Because of this, performance per watt — a metric that can’t be gamed, only earned through real-world results — is the foundation for AI factories.  As agentic AI drives token demand [&
